records.ety: [F. murmure: cf. L. murmur. CF. Murmur, v. i.]

defs: A low, confused, and indistinct sound, like that of running water.
A complaint half suppressed, or uttered in a low, muttering voice.
quotes: Some discontents there are, some idle murmurs.

records.ety: [F. murmurer, L. murmurare, murmurari, fr. murmur murmur; cf. Gr. <?/ to roar and boil, said of water, Skr. marmara a rustling sound; prob. of imitative origin.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Murmured (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Murmuring.]

defs: To make a low continued noise, like the hum of bees, a stream of water, distant waves, or the wind in a forest.
quotes: They murmured as doth a swarm of bees.

defs: To utter complaints in a low, half-articulated voice; to feel or express dissatisfaction or discontent; to grumble; -- often with at or against.
quotes: And all the children of Israel murmured against Moses and against Aaron.
Neither murmur ye, as some of them also murmured.

defs: To utter or give forth in low or indistinct words or sounds; as, to murmur tales.
quotes: The people murmured such things concerning him.
